strategy_name: Optimize Singapore Fab 12i P3 / No New External Expansion
strategic_rationale: Fab 12i P3 is the existing committed execution path with the clearest UMC control, customer qualification path, and capital-allocation logic.
decision_supported: Focus on Singapore Fab 12i P3 execution; defer new non-Singapore expansion.
objective_evidence: UMC already operates Fab 12i in Singapore.
Singapore has an established wafer fabrication, equipment, logistics, and semiconductor talent base.
External alternatives have not passed customer, partner economics, incentive, or ROIC gates.
blockers: None for continuing P3 execution focus.
External options would distract capital and management attention before validation gates are cleared.
next_action: Continue P3 ramp, customer qualification, utilization, margin, and capex tracking before opening external Stage 1 workstreams.

strategic_rationale: Malaysia has the strongest non-Singapore Packaging / OSAT ecosystem signal, especially Penang and Kulim, but ecosystem depth does not prove UMC customer demand or acceptable economics.
decision_supported: Supports monitoring and partner/customer diligence, not investment approval.
objective_evidence: Malaysia accounts for about 13% of global back-end semiconductor activity.
Penang ecosystem includes Intel, Infineon, AMD, Micron, ASE, AT&S, and local design / OSAT firms.
National Semiconductor Strategy and Penang / Kulim cluster evidence support ecosystem relevance.
blockers: UMC customer overlap not validated.
Packaging attach-rate not validated.
Partner economics, incentives, capex / opex, and ROIC not validated.
required_validation: Named UMC wafer flows requiring Malaysia packaging or test
Attach-rate, revenue model, incentive package, and ROIC
next_action: Build customer-overlap map, partner shortlist, attach-rate model, incentive view, and ROIC model before any Stage 1 proposal.

strategic_rationale: Vietnam has EMS and electronics manufacturing relevance, but the case for UMC local customer engineering remains proxy-based.
decision_supported: Monitor for customer-support demand; do not launch a hub without UMC internal customer validation.
objective_evidence: Amkor and Intel have major Vietnam back-end / assembly operations.
Samsung anchors a large electronics manufacturing cluster.
Government semiconductor talent initiatives are emerging.
blockers: Named UMC customer workflows not validated.
FAE workload and revenue mechanism not validated.
Semiconductor-specific talent depth remains a data gap.
required_validation: Named customer overlap in Vietnam EMS clusters
Cost and support model versus Singapore coverage
next_action: Map EMS-linked UMC customers and support workflows that require local engineering rather than regional account coverage.

strategic_rationale: Thailand has automotive, industrial, power, and electronics demand signals relevant to mature-node specialty processes, but UMC-specific revenue is unvalidated.
decision_supported: Use as a demand-sensing watchlist, not an expansion recommendation.
objective_evidence: Thailand has automotive and industrial manufacturing depth.
Eastern Economic Corridor policy targets electronics and advanced industry.
Analog Devices and UTAC provide semiconductor-related presence signals.
blockers: Customer overlap not validated.
Revenue mechanism not validated.
Specialty-process demand by product family not validated.
required_validation: Named automotive, power, and industrial accounts
next_action: Validate automotive, power, and industrial account overlap with Fab 12i and UMC specialty product families.

strategic_rationale: The Philippines has electronics, assembly/test, and English-language support strengths, but the UMC role must be specific and workflow-backed.
decision_supported: Keep as limited watchlist for support, account operations, quality triage, or ATP-related monitoring.
objective_evidence: OECD identifies a semiconductor ecosystem in the Philippines.
Texas Instruments and Analog Devices have Philippine semiconductor operations.
CALABARZON, Clark, and Manila provide electronics and logistics corridors.
blockers: Support role may be generic unless tied to Fab 12i customer workflows.
Semiconductor-specific support talent depth requires validation.
Infrastructure resilience varies by location.
required_validation: Escalation model into Fab 12i and Taiwan engineering
next_action: Define whether the opportunity is account operations, quality triage, FAE support, ATP support, or documentation support.

strategic_rationale: Indonesia has long-term market, policy, and IC design signals, but those signals do not yet imply manufacturing or customer-support readiness for UMC.
decision_supported: Monitor policy and design ecosystem development; do not approve capex or Stage 1 operating setup.
objective_evidence: Indonesia has large domestic automotive, EV, consumer, and industrial demand potential.
Batam provides electronics and logistics adjacency.
Policy and investment institutions are active but broad.
blockers: Customer demand not named.
Partner maturity not validated.
Utilities and execution complexity remain blockers for manufacturing.
required_validation: Named fabless, EMS, or end-customer demand
next_action: Monitor IC design, policy, and end-market development for evidence of UMC-relevant mature-node demand.

strategic_rationale: A new non-Singapore foundry or manufacturing expansion is capital intensive and has not cleared anchor customer, power / water, talent, incentive, or ROIC gates.
decision_supported: Gate out foundry expansion outside Singapore for now.
objective_evidence: Singapore already provides UMC's controlled regional wafer-fab anchor.
Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, Philippines, and Indonesia evidence is stronger for OSAT, EMS, support, policy, or demand sensing than for UMC foundry investment readiness.
No non-Singapore option has validated customer commitments, utility envelope, incentive package, capex / opex, and ROIC.
blockers: Anchor customer commitments not validated.
Power, water, land, permitting, and ESG readiness not validated.
Engineering talent, capex / opex, utilization, and payback not validated.
required_validation: Anchor customer take-or-pay or equivalent demand signal
Site-level power, water, land, permitting, and incentives
Full capex / opex, utilization, and ROIC model
next_action: Do not spend Stage 1 resources on non-Singapore foundry expansion until anchor-customer, utility, incentive, and ROIC gates are materially validated.
